verdicts.reasoning: The homepage describes Gladly as a customer service platform that centralizes customer conversations across channels, which aligns with the recorded description.
The official REST API documentation confirms that Gladly uses HTTP Basic Authentication with an API token, supporting the recorded authentication methods.
The help center documentation indicates that developers can self-generate API tokens from the product UI, confirming the recorded value.
The API reference lists many REST endpoints, indicating broad REST coverage, which supports the recorded API surface value.
The availability of public REST documentation and self-serve API token generation confirms that integration and development are feasible today.
